name: Pudina | Indications, Pharmacology, Dosage, Side Effects & other Generic info with Available Brand names in Bangladesh
indications: Intestinal inflammation, Irritable bowels, Hyperacidity, Abdominal pain, Indigestion.
composition: Each tablet contains 125 mg dry leaf extract of Mentha piperita other excipients as needed.
pharmacology: Pudina is a natural formulation with Pudina, known for soothing intestinal inflammation and relieving IBS symptoms. It helps reduce hyperacidity, abdominal pain, and improves digestion.Mentha piperita contains a variety of chemical compounds, including Menthofuran, Menthol, and 1,8-Cineole.Menthofuran: Menthofuran can reduce the total acidity of gastric secretions and increase their pH, which may help protect the gastric mucosa.Menthol: Menthol has been shown to protect against gastric ulcers potentially by reducing inflammation, increasing mucus production, and modulating prostaglandin E2 (PGE2) level.1,8-Cineole: 1,8-cineole may protect against gastric damage, including ethanol-induced injury, by acting as a cytoprotective agent that increases gastric mucus. It can inhibit gastric juice volume and total acid output, suggesting a role in managing acid-related gastritis. It may promote the healing of gastric ulcers, potentially by increasing the levels of mucus in the gastric.
dosage_&amp;_administration: 1 tablet 1-2 times daily or as per directed by the Registered Physicians.
contraindications: No report is available on contraindication. It may be happened in patients who are hypersensitive to any of its ingredients.
side_effects: No side effects were observed at prescribed doses.
pregnancy_&amp;_lactation: The safety of this tablet in pregnancy has not been studied. Therefore, it should be used with caution during pregnancy as per directed by the Registered Physicians.
storage_conditions: Keep out of reach of children. Keep away from direct sunlight. Store in a cool and dry place.
